message:
fix: harden five concurrency primitives against subtle correctness bugs

Five independent fixes to runtime concurrency primitives discovered
during fresh-eyes audit work:

src/sync/mutex.rs — extract cleanup_waiter() helper:
  Three different drop/cancel/poison paths each duplicated the
  waiter-removal-and-baton-passing logic, with subtle inconsistencies
  in who was eligible to wake the next waiter.  The new helper:
  - removes the waiter from the queue if present
  - only wakes the next waiter when the current waiter held the head
    of the queue OR had been dequeued (holds the baton)
  - never wakes when a waiter that was deeper in the queue drops,
    avoiding spurious wakes that could lead to lost wakeups elsewhere

src/runtime/reactor/token.rs — handle generation overflow in TokenSlab:
  When a slot's generation counter reached MAX_GENERATION, the previous
  code wrapped to 0 and returned the slot to the free list — making it
  reachable by stale tokens whose generation also wrapped.  Now slots
  whose generation reaches MAX_GENERATION are marked permanently
  unusable (next_free = FREE_LIST_END) and never returned to the free
  list, eliminating the ABA-class bug.  Applied uniformly to remove(),
  clear(), and retain().

src/channel/mpsc.rs — remove dead reservation-take branch:
  Reserve::drop had a branch claiming a stale waiter (not in the queue,
  channel still alive) could still hold a pre-granted reservation and
  needed to release it.  Tracing shows this is unreachable — anyone
  removed from the queue without being granted is by definition stale,
  and reserved counts are only incremented for queue entries.  Removing
  the dead branch eliminates a path that could double-decrement
  inner.reserved.

src/channel/watch.rs — fix race in Receiver::poll_changed:
  Three correctness improvements for the changed() future:
  1. Move the version check ahead of the sender-dropped check so a
     fresh value is delivered even if the sender drops in the same
     poll cycle (previously: sender drop was checked first, swallowing
     a pending update).
  2. After registering the waiter, check the version one more time —
     a value sent between our initial check and waker registration
     would otherwise be missed until the next poll.
  3. On the post-register fast path, store false into the local waiter
     flag so the subsequent drop does not take the waiters lock.
  4. ChangedFuture::drop also clears the waiter flag before unlocking
     so a follow-up changed() does not re-take the lock unnecessarily.

src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs — reorder timeout and progress polls:
  RaceConnections::poll previously checked the overall timeout before
  draining in-flight connection attempts.  A connection that completed
  in the same poll cycle as the deadline expiring would be discarded
  in favor of a timeout error.  Now in-flight progress is drained
  first; the overall timeout is only consulted after no in-flight
  connection has completed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
